mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS below its prior 3-month average lowers the discount rate applied to equities and supports higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with positive forward equity performance after accounting for inflation.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines versus its 3-month average but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising faster than the prior 3-month average and FEDFUNDS is above 5%, tighter real policy conditions compress equity valuations and predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 3-month average and FEDFUNDS > 5%
failure_condition: If the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS tightening condition is triggered but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases, the mechanism is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor income prospects and weaker risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect should be strongest when payroll growth is also slowing.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock episodes are followed by positive SPX 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases or if the average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in RSAFS indicates weakening consumer demand and near-term revenue pressure for large-cap firms, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if sales weaken without any accompanying market drawdown.
failure_condition: If RSAFS falls versus its prior month but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of such observations, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST above its 6-month average marks improving housing demand and cyclical growth expectations, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if housing strength does not lead broad equity gains on the stated horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if above-average HOUST readings are not followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO relative to its 3-month average signals stronger industrial output and better profit growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should weaken if output gains are not broad-based across the economy.
failure_condition: If INDPRO improves but SPX 20-day forward returns remain non-positive in more than 55% of observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while UNRATE is falling, the economy is expanding with improving utilization and labor absorption, creating a positive equity return signal over the next 60 trading days. Either series alone is insufficient; the joint improvement is required.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month average and UNRATE falls over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-up and UNRATE-down condition does not produce positive SPX 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-rate-hike stabilization phase, a stable CPIAUCSL paired with improving RSAFS indicates disinflation without demand collapse, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails in active tightening because discount-rate pressure dominates.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL is stable or slowing and RSAFS improves, but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of cases, reject the hypothesis.
